sourceMovie.title: Who Am I?
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Who Am I? (1998) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jackie Chan's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Rundown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Who Am I? clicked for you, The Rundown (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Who Am I??
faq.answer: Try The Rundown, Police Story 4: First Strike, Hatari!—each shares DNA with Who Am I? while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Who Am I??
faq.answer: Who Am I? (1998) is best described as Adventure, Action, Comedy, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Who Am I? worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
